Requires public notice and public engagement when a general hospital seeks to close entirely or a unit that provides maternity, mental health or substance use care
Relates to prohibiting the exclusion of coverage for losses or damages caused by exposure to lead-based paint
Relates to the medical aid in dying act
Relates to traffic regulations of parking or standing vehicles in cities having a population in excess of one million
Relates to medical use of psilocybin; appropriation
Enacts the "Dignity Not Detention" act
Authorizes voting by incarcerated people
Establishes an unemployment bridge program; establishes digital ad tax act; appropriation
Relates to providing a civil action for deprivation of rights
Provides for minimum wage requirements for miscellaneous industry workers and establishes a one fair wage reimbursement credit
Establishes separate taxes on inheritance income and on gift income
Enacts the "consumer and small business protection act"
Relates to licensure requirements for fiscal intermediaries; repealer
Allows the granting of academic credit at city or state operated institutions to veterans
Relates to rebates for stock transfer tax paid; repealer
Enacts the supportive housing modernization act
Relates to the labeling of mifepristone, misoprostol, and their generic alternatives
Enacts into law components of legislation that relate to live event ticket sales
Relates to paratransit services
Relates to eligibility for home care services under medicaid; repealer
Prohibits the knowing and reckless promotion of unlawful or false material and providing remedies for the violation of such prohibition
Raises the tax rate on corporate income; increases the state conformity to federal taxation of corporate profit shifting; and imposes an additional tax on individual business income
Establishes the teachers' fossil fuel divestment act
Prohibits the use of chemical weapons and kinetic energy munitions on civilian populations
Establishes the right to legal counsel in immigration court proceedings and provides for the administration thereof
Enacts the democracy preservation act
Enables appellate review of the fairness and appropriateness of an imposed criminal sentence
Enacts the Empowering People in Rights Enforcement (EMPIRE) Worker Protection Act
Enacts the rider representation act relating to membership on the board of the metropolitan transportation authority
Relates to providing universal school meals to students
Relates to risk retention groups and commuter vans, pre-arranged for-hire vehicles, or accessible vehicles
Relates to correctional facility visits by the correctional association
Relates to parole eligibility for certain incarcerated persons age fifty-five or older
Enacts the crash victims bill of rights
Relates to assistance for burial arrangements
Prohibits the use of leg-gripping traps
Establishes a state SNAP minimum benefit program
Requires high schools to provide a course in financial literacy and requires students to complete such course as a condition of graduation
Grants the legislature to have an equal role with the governor in the budget process
Directs the department of social services to establish a centralized homelessness crisis response data system
Establishes a school air conditioning system grant program; makes an appropriation therefor
Enacts the "gender identity respect, dignity and safety act"
Prohibits the use of intoxication of the victim as a defense in sex crimes
Relates to developing criteria and guidelines for state designated arts and cultural districts
Relates to certain jury awards in employment discrimination actions
Relates to allowances for the costs of diapers
Requires a mortgagor to provide notice of imminent foreclosure
Authorizes certain persons confined in institutions operated by the department of corrections and community supervision to apply for a sentence reduction
Relates to a review of reimbursement adequacy for early intervention services
Relates to fair pricing for low-complexity, routine medical care
Establishes the "protect our privacy (POP) act" to impose limitations on the use of drones for law enforcement purposes
Relates to consumer litigation funding
Relates to conducting a study on various approaches to increasing broadband access within this state
Relates to prohibiting the use of chemical agents by police officers against minors
Establishes the early learning child care act
Relates to the statute of limitations of actions related to certain sex trafficking offenses
Requires a 120-day warning period for violations at certain veterans' organization facilities
Establishes a billionaire mark-to-market tax
Requires anti-bias training for every medical student, medical resident and physician assistant student in the state
Eliminates court surcharges and fees; repealer
Establishes a temporary New York craft beverage permit
Relates to the resolution of certain claims by certain employees
Relates to mortgage loan servicers
Relates to school climate and codes of conduct on school property and disciplinary action following violation of such codes of conduct; and makes conforming amendments
Establishes an alternative resolution program for service members and veterans accused of certain felonies
Relates to procedures required for the custodial interrogation of children
Requires child protective services to orally and in writing disclose certain information to parents and caretakers who are the subject of a child protective services investigation
Relates to flexible working arrangements for parents and legal guardians when day care centers and schools are closed during a state or local disaster emergency
Enacts the "open shelves act"
Establishes the veterans service organizations fund

Expands the eligibility period for indigent legal service attorneys to receive certain loan forgiveness and increases loan reimbursement for certain attorneys who work in legal services with indigent clients
Relates to the mandatory coverage of hearing aids by insurers and other organizations
Relates to findings of the state board of parole necessary for discretionary release of incarcerated individuals on parole
Establishes an indirect source review for certain warehouse operations

Facilitates appellate review of rulings that implicate issues of public concern
Establishes the New York state first home savings program to authorize first time home buyers to establish savings accounts to buy their first home
Prohibits the recording of conveyances suspected of being fraudulent
Establishes the "Maternal Health, Dignity and Consent act"
Establishes a full year youth and young adult employment immersion program
Establishes the "earned time act"
Prohibits residential landlords from charging tenants with fees for the payment of rent through an automated clearing house or online payment system
Establishes the end predatory home flipping act

_prov.source: New York State Senate, Open Legislation API
_prov.method: Session 2025 Senate bills (ceremonial resolutions excluded). 'Prime sponsor' = Myrie is prime sponsor; 'Co-sponsor' = he is a listed co-sponsor. Date shown is the introduction date; subject tags are keyword-matched on the bill title and summary.
_prov.caveats: Status is the latest recorded action ('In Senate Committee' = introduced, not yet advanced). Subject tags are automated approximations. He co-sponsors far more bills than he leads.
